New in Postman: APIs now meet you where you work
Inbox

The Postman Team <notifications@mail.postman.com> Unsubscribe
01:44 (8 minutes ago)
to me

 

Hi Isha,

 

API work has always lived a little apart from everything else: separate from your code, your Git repo, and your CI pipeline. Today, that changes.

 

We're launching a new Postman where your collections, specs, tests, and mocks live alongside your code on your local filesystem and in Git. Now everything is versioned, reviewable through pull requests, and runs the same locally and in CI using the Postman CLI. 

 

We've also shipped Agent Mode, which understands context across your specs, tests, collections, and mocks. Describe the change you want, and Agent Mode handles it accurately the first time. Every update it makes is also versioned and traceable, so you're reducing manual work without giving up visibility or control.

 

Plus, with other new capabilities, you can now:

Organize requests across HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, MCP, MQTT, WebSockets, and AI in a single collection. This means you have one place to validate behavior across protocols, rather than jumping between tools.
Run performance tests directly from your collections, so concurrency and load issues surface before release.
We've also introduced a new Solo plan for individual developers, with additional AI and automation capabilities built in.

7 of 984
